reforms.description: In 2003, Switzerland proposed to provide public support to child care through targeted subsidies or tax expenditures, aimed at increasing female participation in the labor market.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing child care support is expected to increase female labor participation, thereby contributing to economic growth through a more inclusive workforce.
reforms.status_evidence: Public support for child care was proposed to enhance female labor participation.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform addresses a significant barrier to female labor participation, which is crucial for economic growth, but it is not a comprehensive overhaul of the system.
reforms.source_quote: Provide public support to child care through targeted child care subsidies or through tax expenditures.
